notes.note: The reference is obscure: Athamas may be a slave or freedman of Atticus's whose death or illness Atticus had reported, prompting Cicero's words of consolation. The identity is uncertain and no firm prosopographical fit exists.

Alexis was Atticus's literate slave-secretary, whom Cicero repeatedly likens to his own beloved freedman Tiro for his diligence and charm. Here Cicero offers to take Alexis into his own household if plague threatens.
Renders the Greek "epidēmion," a sickness endemic to a place; Cicero fears an outbreak on Atticus's hill at Rome and offers the empty upper storey of his own house as a refuge.
